revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:日枝神社 (山形市)]] {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Hie Jinja | image = [[File:211029 Risshaku-ji Yamagata Yamagata pref Japan07s5.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|38|18|44.5|N|140|26|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|qid=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata (city)}} Yamadera 4449-4 | coordinates = {{Coord|38|18|44.5|N|140|26|12.8|E|display=inline,title}} | enshrined deity = {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamatsukami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} | founding = {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|860|en|860|qid=Q24714|lt=860 AD|1=860}}) | shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | alias = Yamadera Hie Jinja | annual festival = May 17[http://www.kankou.yamagata.yamagata.jp/db/cgi-bin/search/search.cgi?panel=detail&d01=95958850220120 Yamadera Hie Jinja Sannō Festival] - Yamagata City Tourism Association[https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202405/17/kj_2024051700542.php "Orhya!" Mikoshi Sprints — Yamadera Hie Jinja Sannō Festival] - {{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11468452}} 2024/5/17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=18|lat_sec=44.5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=12.8|label=Yamadera
Hie Jinja|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Hie Jinja''' (日枝神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yamadera, {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|qid=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata (city)}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It is also known by its geographical name as '''Yamadera Hie Jinja''' (山寺日枝神社). ==Overview== It is said that in 860 AD ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2), when Ennin, the third head priest of {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism|1=Tiantai}}, established the temple {{ill|Yama-dera|en|Yama-dera|qid=Q1760419|lt=Risshaku-ji|1=Yama-dera}} (Yamadera) on Mount Hōju, he, following the example of {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en|Enryaku-ji|qid=Q917195|lt=Enryaku-ji|1=Enryaku-ji}} on Mount Hiei, enshrined a branch spirit from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}. This act is regarded as the founding of the shrine.[https://hie348.wixsite.com/yamaderahiejinja/yuisyo History] - Yamadera Hie Jinja From the beginning, it was venerated as {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Ōmiya Daigongen|ja|大宮大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with the establishment of the twenty-one Sannō shrines, it became a major stronghold of {{ill|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|en|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|qid=Q4407806|lt=Sannō Shintō|1=Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} in the Tōhoku region. In 1870 AD ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3), under the Shinto-Buddhist separation edicts ({{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}}/{{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏判然令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}}) of the Meiji government, it was separated from {{ill|Yama-dera|en|Yama-dera|qid=Q1760419|lt=Risshaku-ji|1=Yama-dera}} and renamed Hie Jinja as the guardian shrine of Yamadera Village, acquiring the Rank of a former village shrine. Thus, the role of serving as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) for over a thousand years since its founding came to an end. ==Enshrined Deity== * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamatsukami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} ==Footnotes== {{reflist}} ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Yama-dera|en|Yama-dera|qid=Q1760419|lt=Risshaku-ji|1=Yama-dera}} ==External Links== * [https://hie348.wixsite.com/yamaderahiejinja/ Yamadera Hie Jinja] - Official Website * {{Instagram|yamadera_hiejinja|Yamadera Hie Jinja}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社 (山形市)|version=103113725|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社 (山形市)]] [[ja:日枝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134888881}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamagata, Yamagata]] [[Category:History of Yamagata, Yamagata]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
